# EdgeBase PHP SDK (`edgebase/sdk`)

`edgebase/sdk` is the broad Composer entry point for the EdgeBase PHP SDKs.

It bundles both the trusted admin surface and the lower-level core primitives. Use the narrower package docs when the user explicitly wants `edgebase/admin` or `edgebase/core`.

The package names `edgebase/sdk`, `edgebase/admin`, and `edgebase/core` are the intended public Composer names, but the current repository layout still needs a Packagist-compatible publish path before those installs work from Packagist.

## Package Boundaries

- `edgebase/sdk`
  Broad Composer install for PHP server-side usage
- `edgebase/admin`
  Trusted Service Key operations such as admin auth, database admin access, SQL, push, analytics, functions, KV, D1, and Vectorize
- `edgebase/core`
  Lower-level HTTP client, database refs, storage helpers, field operations, shared errors, and room primitives

## Canonical Guidance

- PHP is server-side only in this repository.
- Prefer `EdgeBase\Admin\AdminClient` for trusted application code.
- Keep Service Keys on trusted servers only.
- Use PHP property and method shapes as implemented, not JavaScript promise-style APIs.
- Reach for `edgebase/core` only when building wrappers or lower-level integrations.

## Docs

- SDK Overview: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks
- Admin SDK Reference: https://edgebase.fun/docs/admin-sdk/reference
- Database Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/database/admin-sdk
- Storage: https://edgebase.fun/docs/storage/upload-download
- Native Resources: https://edgebase.fun/docs/server/native-resources

## Install

- planned public package name: `composer require edgebase/sdk`
- current repository-first usage: consume this monorepo with Composer path/VCS wiring until Packagist publishing is wired
